Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Building a Blog with Elixir and Phoenix 28 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. OpenTelemetry profiles enters public alpha 66 upvotes, 5 comments
  3. Why so many control rooms were seafoam green (2025) 88 upvotes, 9 comments
  4. Personal Encyclopedias 693 upvotes, 141 comments
  5. Show HN: Claude skill that evaluates B2B vendors by talking to their AI agents 41 upvotes, 4 comments
  6. Optimization lessons from a Minecraft structure locator 56 upvotes, 7 comments
  7. Obsolete Sounds 166 upvotes, 34 comments
  8. Interoperability Can Save the Open Web (2023) 139 upvotes, 38 comments
  9. Running Tesla Model 3's computer on my desk using parts from crashed cars 798 upvotes, 279 comments
  10. From zero to a RAG system: successes and failures 224 upvotes, 69 comments
  11. My home network observes bedtime with OpenBSD and pf 69 upvotes, 18 comments
  12. SpaceStarCarz KoolWheelz Paper Models 25 upvotes, 4 comments
  13. Stripe Projects: Provision and manage services from the CLI 29 upvotes, 6 comments
  14. Colibri – chat platform built on the AT Protocol for communities big and small 32 upvotes, 8 comments
  15. HyperAgents: Self-referential self-improving agents 31 upvotes, 8 comments
  16. Shell Tricks That Make Life Easier (and Save Your Sanity) 404 upvotes, 190 comments
  17. Light on Glass: Why do you start making a game engine? 25 upvotes, 6 comments
  18. Moving from GitHub to Codeberg, for lazy people 381 upvotes, 185 comments
  19. Intel Announces Arc Pro B70 and Arc Pro B65 GPUs 101 upvotes, 42 comments
  20. My minute-by-minute response to the LiteLLM malware attack 138 upvotes, 62 comments
  21. Niche Museums 90 upvotes, 40 comments
  22. End of "Chat Control": EU parliament stops mass surveillance 375 upvotes, 204 comments
  23. Doom entirely from DNS records 30 upvotes, 10 comments
  24. Swift 6.3 277 upvotes, 168 comments
  25. ARC-AGI-3 479 upvotes, 308 comments
  26. Show HN: Orloj – agent infrastructure as code (YAML and GitOps) 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  27. Optimizing a lock-free ring buffer 63 upvotes, 55 comments
  28. AI users whose lives were wrecked by delusion 125 upvotes, 128 comments
  29. Ant Chat 13 upvotes, 7 comments
  30. Ashby (YC W19) Is Hiring Engineers Who Make Product Decisions 0 upvotes, 0 comments
